title: checkout-service CrashLoopBackOff after deploy
description: checkout-service pods entered CrashLoopBackOff within minutes of a routine deploy. kubectl logs --previous showed OOMKilled, exit code 137.
root_cause: The new deploy bumped a background worker's batch size, increasing per-pod memory usage beyond the configured 512Mi limit.
resolution: Rolled back to the previous deployment revision. Follow-up: raise the memory limit and add a memory usage alert before the next batch-size change.

title: db-primary-02 disk usage critical, writes failing
description: Alert fired for disk usage above 90% on db-primary-02. Application writes began failing with disk-full errors shortly after.
root_cause: Old WAL (write-ahead log) segments were not being cleaned up after a replication lag issue earlier in the week, silently consuming disk space over several days.
resolution: Manually archived and removed old WAL segments, freeing 40GB. Follow-up: alert on WAL segment count, not just raw disk percentage.

title: payment-api intermittent 500s, connection pool exhausted
description: payment-api began returning 500 errors intermittently under normal traffic. Logs showed 'connection pool exhausted' from the database client.
root_cause: A recently added reporting job opened long-lived database connections without releasing them, starving the shared connection pool.
resolution: Killed the leaking reporting job's connections and added a connection timeout. Follow-up: move the reporting job to its own dedicated connection pool.

title: auth-service DNS resolution failures after node pool upgrade
description: auth-service pods intermittently failed to resolve internal service DNS names right after a Kubernetes node pool upgrade.
root_cause: The new node pool's kubelet was using a different CoreDNS configuration that hadn't propagated the internal search domain correctly.
resolution: Restarted CoreDNS pods to force a config refresh. Follow-up: add a DNS resolution health check to the node pool upgrade runbook.

title: api-gateway TLS certificate expired, all traffic returning 526
description: External traffic to api-gateway began failing with 526 (invalid SSL certificate) errors at exactly midnight UTC.
root_cause: The TLS certificate's auto-renewal job had been silently failing for three weeks due to an unrelated permissions change, and no alert existed for renewal failures.
resolution: Manually renewed and deployed the certificate. Follow-up: alert explicitly on certificate expiry date, not just on renewal job success/failure.

title: worker-queue memory usage climbing steadily over 6 hours before OOM
description: worker-queue pods showed a slow, steady memory increase over about 6 hours, eventually hitting their memory limit and getting OOMKilled.
root_cause: A recently introduced caching layer in the worker was never evicting old entries, causing an unbounded memory leak under sustained load.
resolution: Rolled back the caching change and added a TTL-based eviction policy before re-deploying. Follow-up: add a memory-growth-rate alert, not just an absolute threshold.

title: checkout-service returning 500s, unrelated to the OOM incident
description: checkout-service returned elevated 500 errors for about 15 minutes. No recent deploy, no OOM kills observed in this window.
root_cause: A downstream inventory-service experienced a brief network partition, and checkout-service had no timeout configured on that call, so requests piled up.
resolution: Added a request timeout and circuit breaker on the inventory-service client. Follow-up: audit other services for missing timeouts on downstream calls.

title: db-replica-01 disk usage high but not yet critical
description: Disk usage on db-replica-01 reached 78% and is trending upward, similar to the pattern seen before the db-primary-02 incident.
root_cause: Under investigation - replication lag has been intermittently spiking, which may be causing similar WAL segment buildup as INC-1002.
resolution: Proactively archiving old WAL segments and monitoring replication lag closely. Follow-up: apply INC-1002's WAL-segment-count alert to all replicas, not just primaries.
